pubmed_links: [Hashimoto et al. (2012) Genetic and functional analysis of the gene encoding GAP-43 in schizophrenia](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22138049/); [Ohnishi et al. (2021) Resequencing and association analysis of GAP43 with autism spectrum disorder and schizophrenia in a Japanese population](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1750946721000040); [Matsumura et al. (2024) An unstable variant of GAP43 leads to neurodevelopmental deficiency](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39738362/)
summary: GAP43 encodes a presynaptic phosphoprotein critical for axonal growth and synaptic plasticity; altered GAP43 expression has been repeatedly reported in postmortem brains of individuals with schizophrenia and bipolar disorder, and heterozygous knockout mice display autistic-like social and behavioral deficits, with rare de novo variants linked to neurodevelopmental disorders including intellectual disability. (emerging evidence)
